title: Spectrophotometric Determination of Iron (III) with  1-Hydroxyzanthone
author: Brahm Dev
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: 1- Hydroxyxanthone has been used for the spectrophotometric determination of iron (III). With iron (III) this reagent forms a yellowish green complex, soluble in 50% ethanol (v/v) .  The complex has been studied. spectrophotometrically and its molar composition determined. The complex obeys Lambert-Beer's law at 425 m \(\mu\)  within the concentration range 1.1-11.0 p.p.m. of iron. The intensity of the colour does not vary between   \(p\)  H 1.8 and 2.3. The molar composition of iron (III)-1-hydroxyxanthone shows that it contains iron and the xanthone in the molar ratio of 1:2. The limits of interference due to various cations and anions in the deter­mination have been given.
